{"step_by_step_thinking": "Option A states that according to ACOG, the latent phase starts after 3-4 cm cervical dilatation, but they are planning to increase it to 5 cm. This is supported by Document [23], which mentions that ACOG supports using Zhang's curve data and recommends defining a prolonged latent phase as lasting more than 16 hours, regardless of parity, and that they are planning to increase the threshold to 5 cm. Option B is incorrect because the latent phase does not begin at the end of the active phase; it is part of the first stage of labor but precedes the active phase. Option C is correct as patients can present with false labor due to mild cramps, which is mentioned in several documents. Option D is also correct as the latent phase starts with contractions of the uterus, as stated in Document [20]. Therefore, the statement that is not true is option B.", "answer_choice": "B"}
